results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL IS TO ADDRESS AIR POLLUTION AND SUBSEQUENT HEALTH IMPACTS WHILE CONSIDERING THE CHALLENGES FOR ENERGY, MOBILITY, ACCESSIBILITY, AND EQUITABILITY IN FIFTEEN UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES IN THE COLUMBUS METROPOLITAN AREA (CMA). ACTIVITIES:THE PROJECT WILL 1) PARTNER WITH UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES IN THE CMA TO UNDERSTAND THEIR NEEDS FOR ELECTRIFICATION AND MOBILITY AND EVALUATE THE EFFECTIVENESS OF CURRENT EFFORTS; 2) DEVELOP STRATEGIES TO GUIDE TRANSITION INVESTMENT AND DEPLOYMENT TO IMPROVE HEALTH, ENVIRONMENTAL, AND SOCIAL CONDITIONS OVER TIME FOR THE CMA COMMUNITIES ACROSS THE URBAN-EXURBAN-RURAL GRADIENT; AND 3) UNDERSTAND HOW KNOWLEDGE, PERCEPTION, AND VALUES VARY, AND DESIGN AND EVALUATE INFORMATIONAL INTERVENTIONS THAT INFORM INDIVIDUAL AND DOMICILE-LEVEL DECISIONS.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL RESEARCH REPORTS. OTHER EXPECTED DELIVERABLES INCLUDE: COMMUNITY ASSESSMENTS AND TOOLKITS TO SUPPORT LOCAL AND REGIONAL DECISION-MAKERS ON TRANSITIONS TO ELECTRIFIED ENERGY AND MOBILITY; AND ELECTRICITY AND MOBILITY INFRASTRUCTURE PLANNING WHITE PAPERS AND PRESENTATIONS. EXPECTED OUTCOMES ARE TESTED INFORMATIONAL INTERVENTIONS FOR USE IN HELPING MARGINALIZED COMMUNITIES UNDERSTAND THE DECISIONS FACING THEIR HOUSEHOLDS DURING ELECTRIFIED ENERGY AND MOBILITY TRANSITIONS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES IN THE COLUMBUS METROPOLITAN AREA AS WELL AS RESEARCHERS WORKING WITH SIMILAR COMMUNITIES TO ADDRESS AIR QUALITY, PUBLIC HEALTH, AND COST CONCERNS ASSOCIATED WITH ELECTRIFIED ENERGY AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES.
